forecast-period.text: A high pressure system currently over the eastern border will drift into NSW as a weak trough skims the southeast today and into Wednesday morning. A new high will move across waters to the south mid-week to be centred over Tasmania on Thursday, continuing to extend a ridge over SA on Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Isolated showers near southern coasts. Patchy morning fog about the Agricultural Areas and frost about the Murray districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Light and variable winds

Isolated showers about southern coastal districts and possibly developing over the Nullarbor Plain. Areas of morning fog and patchy frost about central and eastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average. East to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the far west.

Isolated showers about parts of the southern coastal districts and over the Nullarbor Plain, possibly extending further through the West Coast. Areas of morning fog about the eastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average. East to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the far west.

text.p: Isolated light showers about the southern coasts, possibly also developing about the Flinders and southeastern Pastorals. Patchy morning fog about the Agricultural Areas. East to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the far west.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: less than 2 mm for most districts, 2-5 mm about Kangaroo Island, the Nullarbor and far southeast. Isolated falls of 5-15 mm possible about southeastern coastal fringes.
